title: SOLVING PROBLEMS WITH LORD'S HELP | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Welcome to today's Gita Live session.
It's a very interesting topic today and the topic is
We all encounter challenges, difficulties, trials, tribulations and problems in our life.
The degree may vary from person to person but everyone encounters challenges.
And at times, the challenges and difficulties can be very overwhelming.
We can be completely shattered or we can be completely rattled because of the problems we encounter.
So today's session, we are going to discuss about how we can remain strong, calm
not get completely bewildered when we encounter such problems if we depend on the Lord.
If we seek Lord's protection and guidance to take us through such challenging times.
Before we get into the discussions, let us start the session with prayers.
I request all of you to kindly join me.
We can all fold our hands and offer prayers.
To the Lord for his blessings so that we can have a wonderful session with a lot of takeaways.

Our scriptures give us example of a cat carrying a rat and the same cat carrying a kitten. Many
of us must have seen that when a cat carries a rat, the rat in its mouth, the rat is experiencing
death. It knows that any moment it's going to die. It knows that any moment it's going to die. It's
going to be killed and it will be swallowed. In the same mouth, the kitten is also carried by the
rat. But the kitten, unlike the rat, experiences warmth of mother's love. Same mouth carried by the
same cat, but one is experiencing death, another one is experiencing warmth of mother's love.
Likewise, if that relationship is crystallized, if we have invested time and energy in building
that relationship with the Lord, then even if the devotee goes through some difficulties,
some challenges, he will experience Lord's reciprocation, Lord's protection, Lord's love.
Although superficially, just like the cat carrying the rat or the kitten, superficially it appears,
both of them are going to get killed. Superficially, it may appear that the devotee is not protected
by the Lord. Devotee is also suffering like any ordinary person. But devotee, genuine devotee,
who has evoked that relationship, experiences Lord's love, Lord's protection. So the conviction,
that Nishtha, that Avashe Rakshabhi Krishna, a devotee has that conviction, that Lord will surely
protect me. This is the conviction which even Prahlad Maharaj had. As I mentioned earlier,
he was tormented by his father just because he was a devotee of Lord Shri Krishna. But
he, in spite of all the challenges, all the difficulties he encountered, his faith in
the Lord was unshaken. In fact, Kuntimaharani in the Srimad Bhagavatam, prays to the Lord,
O Lord, let more and more difficulties come. So that it's an impetus for us to remember
you. It's a fact that we tend to remember the Lord when we are in difficulties. And
there is a possibility of we forgetting the Lord when we go through good times. So Kuntimaharani,
Kunti Maharani is saying, oh Lord, let more and more difficulties come. Now that we have got the
kingdom back and you are leaving us, we'll all forget you. So let more and more difficulties
come so that it's an impetus for us to remember you and remembering you means freedom from the
cycle of birth and death. So Kunti Maharani was in knowledge. Unfortunately, we judge
Lord's protection, stay deep in ignorance. And many times we mistake or we wrongly conclude
about Lord's protection, more so when it comes to Lord giving protection to his devotees.
Once there was a Mulla, he was on a vacation and he was having his family, his wife and
was having a meal. And he was having a meal with his wife and he was having a meal with
his wife and he was having a meal with his wife and he was having a meal with his wife.
He was having a boat ride. And on that particular day, the weather was quite stormy, the winds were

tossing the boat. And it appeared that any moment the boat could capsize and they could be in water.
And the life of Mulla started screaming, Help! Help! Help! Obviously, she was quite panic-stricken
because she did not know how to swim. And while she was screaming was
completely anxiety, fearful of death. She saw her husband, Mulla sitting in one corner of
the boat, offering namaz, offering prayers.
Could it be because of the prayers, sincere prayers, because of his relationship with the Lord?
Somehow, the storm subsided miraculously and they could reach the shore.

What kind of person are you? Are you not afraid of death?
